body: PT Shimizu Global Indonesia has sued PT PP Urban, a subsidiary of PT Pembangunan Perumahan TBK, over a debt payment obligation, with two lawsuits filed last week (Case No. 299/PDT.SUS-PKPU/2024/PN Niaga Jkt.Pst and No. 300/PDT.SUS-PKPU/2024/PN Niaga Jkt-Pst). According to PT PP Urban's Director of Finance, Agus Purbianto, the lawsuits are not expected to significantly impact the company's legal or financial condition, or its going concern issues.
title: PT Shimizu Global Indonesia sues PT PP Urban over debt payment obligation suspension.

body: PT Koka Indonesia's stock price plummeted 51.56% to IDR 62 per share, down from its IPO price of IDR 128, with a market capitalization of IDR 177.40 billion. Meanwhile, PT LogisticsPlus International's stock crashed 74% to IDR 26 per share, down from its IPO price of IDR 100, with a market capitalization of IDR 28.60 billion.
title: Koka and LogisticsPlus Shares Plummet After IPO on Indonesia Stock Exchange

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange has suspended PT Bank JTrust Indonesia TBK and PT Jakarta Setiabudi Internasional TBK due to a significant rapid surge in stock prices, with BCIC's stock price soaring 87.5% and JSPT's surging 96.65% over the past 30 trading days. The suspension, effective from today's morning session, aims to protect investors and provide time for market players to reassess their investments based on current information.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Suspends Two Companies Due to Rapid Stock Price Surge for Investor Protection

body: Akra's subsidiary, PT Terminal Energi Primer, is formed to engage in warehouse storage and other energy supply-related activities, alongside PT Usaha Era Pratama Nusantara. Akra has a cash reserve of IDR 4.82 trillion as of June 30, 2024, although the total investment spent on establishing PT Terminal Energi Primer is undisclosed.
title: Akra Forms New Subsidiary PT Terminal Energi Primer for Warehouse Storage and Energy Supply Activities

body: PT Victoria Care Indonesia Tbk will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 23.47 billion, or IDR 35 per share, from its net profit in the first half of 2024, with dividend payment scheduled for November 8, 2024. The company's net profit attributable to the parent entity was IDR 97.87 billion in June 2024, with unrestricted retained earnings of IDR 550.09 billion and total equity of IDR 969.47 billion.
title: PT Victoria Care Indonesia to Distribute IDR 23.47 Billion Interim Dividend for First Half 2024.

body: PT Indonesia Infrastructure Finance (IIF) is targeting IDR 1 trillion in fresh funds from the issuance of Shelf-Registration Bond II Phase II in 2024, part of a larger IDR 3 trillion public offering. The bond issuance, with interest rates ranging from 6.30% to 7.05% per annum and tenors from 370 days to 10 years, will be used to refinance debt and boost working capital, with IDR 600 billion allocated for debt repayment and the remainder for working capital.
title: Indonesia Infrastructure Finance targets IDR 1 trillion from shelf-registration bond issuance in 2024.

body: On October 8, PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk saw the largest sales with 1.14 billion shares sold, resulting in a net volume of -297.64 million shares, while foreign selling volume reached 4.15 billion shares. Meanwhile, foreign investors purchased shares in several companies, including PT Panin Financial Tbk, PT Aneka Tambang Tbk, and PT Timah Tbk, with net volumes ranging from 22.92 million to 59.98 million shares.
title: PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia TBK sees highest sales with 1.14 billion shares sold on Tuesday, October 8.

body: PT Aladin Global Ventures acquired a majority stake in PT Bank Aladin Syariah Tbk, increasing its share portion to 50.62% with a purchase of 5.84 billion shares, while other investors such as PT Iforte Solusi Infotek and Iwa Sukresno Karunia also made notable purchases in various companies. Meanwhile, several companies, including PT Panin Sekuritas Tbk and PT Samuela Tumbuh Bersama, released shares in companies such as PT Elnusa Tbk and PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk.
title: Multiple investors make significant share purchases and sales in various Indonesian companies.

body: PT Pulau Subur Tbk (KOCI) and PT Kokoh Exa Nusantara Tbk (PTPS) have seen significant declines in their stock prices since their IPOs in October 2023, with KOCI's stock price dropping 56.66% to IDR 52 and PTPS's stock price plummeting 144.44% to IDR 81.
title: Two Indonesian stocks, PT Pulau Subur and PT Kokoh Exa Nusantara, experience significant declines in value one year after their initial public offerings.

body: PT Cakra Buana Resources Energi Tbk (CBRE) has terminated its vessel charter contract with PT Habco Trans Maritima Tbk (HTMA) to improve vessel performance and potentially gain higher profits from new clients. The contract termination is expected to have no material impact on operational activities, and CBRE has already received several offers with higher charter values.
title: PT Cakra Buana Resources Energi terminates vessel charter contract with PT Habco Trans Maritima to focus on improving vessel performance and seeking higher profit.

body: PT Petrotech Penta Nusa, a subsidiary of PT Rukun Raharja Tbk, is investing IDR 982.45 billion (USD 63.29 million) in a fuel oil piping project from Tanjung Batu to Samarinda, expected to increase future profits. The investment value represents 39.84% of the company's total equity of USD 158.87 million as of June.
title: Petrotech Penta Nusa Invests IDR 982.45 Billion in Fuel Oil Piping Project in Indonesia.

body: PT Astra Otoparts TBK, an automotive spare part manufacturer, plans to allocate an interim dividend of IDR 57 per share, totaling IDR 274.72 billion, to its shareholders, following a decision made by its board of directors. The company recorded a net profit of IDR 1.01 trillion in the first half of 2024 and retained unrestricted earnings of IDR 9.71 trillion, with a total equity of IDR 14.84 trillion.
title: Astra Otoparts to Allocate IDR 274.72 Billion in Interim Dividend to Shareholders

body: PT Logindo Samudera Makmur Tbk, an oil and natural gas services company, will issue 1.75 billion new shares, equivalent to 30.17% of its fully paid capital, to raise funds for debt repayment, as its current net working capital stands at a deficit of $3.02 million and total liabilities exceed 80% of total assets. The company will seek approval from its extraordinary general meeting of shareholders on November 14, 2024, to proceed with the private placement, aimed at addressing its financial restructuring needs.
title: Logindo Samudera Makmur to Conduct Rights Issue to Raise Capital for Debt Repayment Amid Financial Restructuring

body: Foreign investors sold a net 1 billion shares in the Indonesian market, with significant sales in companies like PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk, PT Bumi Resources Tbk, and PT Bukalapak.com Tbk. Meanwhile, domestic investors purchased shares in companies like PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk, PT Surya Citra Media Tbk, and PT Lippo Karawaci Tbk, with net volumes ranging from 38.88 million to 74.74 million shares.
title: Foreign investors sell 1 billion more shares than they buy in Indonesian market, led by sales of digital and coal mining companies.
